shouldTrigger: Redesign our authentication flow and deploy it safely, but the supported identity providers are undecided.
Move this monolith to services; choose the boundaries with me before implementation.
Delete the legacy customer data after deciding which retention rule applies.
Create a public API, but we have not chosen whether breaking changes are allowed.
Replace our payment provider and decide the rollback and acceptance evidence first.
Turn these conflicting stakeholder notes into one implementation contract.
nearMiss: Fix the typo in README line 12 and run the Markdown linter.
Explain what src/router.mjs does without changing files.
Run the existing unit tests and report failures only.
Rename the local variable oldName to newName in test fixture A.
List the repository's package scripts from package.json.
Update the pinned dependency from 1.2.3 to 1.2.4 and run its existing test.

shouldTrigger: Ship this feature however you think best; production permissions and success criteria are not specified.
Consolidate the two databases, but first determine which system remains authoritative.
Add Grok support without overstating which tools or resume features really work.
Prepare a migration that may invalidate user sessions; agree on tolerated downtime and rollback first.
nearMiss: Add alt text 'DisciplinedRun routing diagram' to docs/router.png.
Summarize the last five commits without editing the repository.
Change the test timeout from 1000 to 1500 milliseconds in test/config.test.mjs.
Format package.json with the repository's existing formatter and show the diff.

outcomeTasks.prompt: Port DisciplinedRun to another provider while preserving safety and honest capability claims.
Plan a destructive data cleanup whose retention authority is not yet known.
Fix one documented typo and run the existing lint command.
